data.title: Helion's Polaris prototype achieves plasma temperatures of 150 MºC and demonstrates deuterium-tritium fusion, accelerating commercial fusion.
data.description: Helion, a fusion energy company based in Washington, revealed that its Polaris prototype has achieved groundbreaking milestones, setting new standards in the fusion industry. It is the first privately developed fusion energy device to successfully showcase measurable deuterium-tritium fusion. The prototype has also reached plasma temperatures of 150 million degrees Celsius (MºC). These achievements mark significant progress towards Helion's goal of establishing fusion energy as a commercially feasible source. This development is unprecedented in the private fusion energy sector.

data.title: Catwalk Ent. Group partners with HK director Tze Yiu Chui for Pan-Asian push, boosting C-pop on global stage.
data.description: On February 13, 2026, in Taipei, a leading Pan-Asian entertainment conglomerate, Catwalk Entertainment Group, revealed a significant partnership with renowned Hong Kong director and screenwriter, Tze Yiu Chui. This collaboration signifies a notable achievement for Catwalk as they continue to expand their presence in the entertainment industry.

data.title: Meta is adding facial recognition to its smart glasses amid US political turmoil, hoping to divert attention from critics.
data.description: Last year, Meta disclosed in an internal memo that the ongoing political instability in the United States could potentially divert attention away from the launch of the new feature.

data.title: Christie's auctioning 3 art pieces - Rothko, Twombly, Joseph Cornell's Surrealist box with a Medici princess - valued at $145M.
data.description: Three iconic artworks, including a Rothko piece, a Twombly creation, and a Surrealist box featuring a Medici princess crafted by Joseph Cornell, are expected to fetch a staggering $145 million at an upcoming auction.

data.title: New Epstein evidence sparks conspiracy theories with millions of documents, fueling speculation & hoaxes.
data.description: The release of countless documents has sparked fresh rumors, hoaxes created by artificial intelligence, and foreign propaganda campaigns.

data.title: Rise in cargo thefts, driven by stolen food and beverages, highlights surge in crimes, according to experts.
data.description: An increase in cargo crimes is being fueled by stolen food and beverages, which are easier to sell compared to expensive electronics, according to truckers, insurers, and industry experts. This rise in thefts is a cause for concern, with reports showing a noticeable spike in incidents. The increased demand for these stolen goods has contributed to the problem, with numbers indicating a significant impact on the transportation and insurance sectors. The trend highlights the need for enhanced security measures to combat this growing issue.

data.title: Allies of RFK Jr. are focusing on states to reverse school vaccine mandates, raising concerns about weakening immunization systems and increasing disease outbreaks.
data.description: Advocates for vaccines are cautioning that these actions could contribute to the erosion of the vaccination system and result in an increase in disease outbreaks.

data.title: Amtrak to introduce biggest train upgrade in 55 years at California factory; largest fleet replacement in history underway.
data.description: Venture into the expansive factory located in California, where Amtrak's biggest fleet replacement in its 55-year existence is being assembled meticulously.

data.title: Venezuela's untapped natural gas reserves present a quick export opportunity, amid geopolitical hurdles, overshadowed by oil.
data.description: A country in South America possesses significant reserves of natural gas that have the potential to be extracted and exported promptly. However, progress in this area has been hindered by geopolitical obstacles.

data.title: Iran uses digital surveillance to hunt down protesters after restoring online services following anti-government protests, targeting attendees with technology.
data.description: Following recent antigovernment demonstrations in Iran, authorities have started restoring certain online services. However, a technological dragnet is being employed to target attendees of the protests. This comes after a wave of protests where thousands of citizens took to the streets to express their grievances. The crackdown has seen over 2,000 people arrested so far, with many facing harsh repercussions for their involvement. The targeted surveillance is part of the government's efforts to suppress dissent and maintain control in the aftermath of the protests.
